---
title: "Exercises from Week 6, Lecture 1: Environments"
subtitle: "SDS 270"
author: "NAME HERE"
format: html
---

## Packages

```{r}
library(rlang)
```

::: {.callout-important}

### Q1

Load in a package of your choice. Then run the following:

```{r, eval = FALSE}
search()
```

Answer the following questions:

- How is the package ordered relative to the rest of the environments?
- If there is more than one package associated with what you loaded: why do you think the developers chose the order they did?


:::

::: {.callout-important}

### Q2

Let’s write a function that returns the environment that runs during its execution. Note that this is **not** the global environment.

```{r, eval = FALSE}
func_env <- function() {
  x <- "what?"
  current_env()
}

env_print(func_env())
```

- What is the parent environment of the execution environment shown above?


:::


## Turn-In

Stage, commit, and push your template file to your SDS 270 repository.
